title: malachra capitata
intro: Malachra capitata, also known as the woolly apple, is a tropical plant species native to Central and South America. The leaves and roots of Malachra capitata are used in traditional medicine, primarily for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The plant's medicinal properties are believed to provide health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating skin conditions. Malachra capitata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in various forms.
